ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Of Hosius, Bishop of Cordova.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Since we have observed that Hosius the Spaniard was present [at the council of Sirmium] against his will, it is necessary to give some brief account of him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: A short time before he had been sent into exile by the intrigues of the Arians: but at the earnest solicitation of those convened at Sirmium, the emperor summoned him thither, wishing that by persuasion, or by compulsion he should give his sanction to their proceedings; for if this could be effected, they considered it would give great authority to their sentiments.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: On this ground, therefore, as I have said, he was most unwillingly obliged to be present: and when he refused to concur with them, stripes and tortures were inflicted on the old man.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Wherefore he was constrained by force to acquiesce in and subscribe to their exposition of the faith.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such was the issue of affairs at that time transacted at Sirmium.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But the emperor Constantius after these things still continued to reside at that place, awaiting there the result of the war against Magnentius.
